objective:
FICHE_STRATEGIE v1
id: breakout_donchian_adx
archetype: breakout_donchian_adx
family: breakout
timeframe: 1h
side: both
indicators:
- donchian(period=25)
- adx(period=25)
- atr(period=14)
entry:
- long: close > donchian.upper and adx.adx > 25
- short: close < donchian.lower and adx.adx > 25
exit:
- condition: cross_any(close, donchian.middle) or adx.adx < 15
risk:
stop_atr_mult: 1.5
tp_atr_mult: 2.5
description: ATR stop-loss (1.5x) and take-profit (2.5x)
constraints:
- no_lookahead: true
- only_registry_indicators: true

pre_reflection.text:
[Pre-reflection] This strategy could be prone to whipsaws as it uses both bullish and bearish momentum signals. If results are poor, I would try to adjust the entry criteria by increasing the minimum ADX reading or changing the cross-over condition to be less sensitive to short-term price fluctuations. Alternatively, I would consider adding a trend-following indicator like RSI to filter out potential false breakouts.
[Backup plan] If performance is unsatisfactory, I will try different parameter adjustments such as higher minimum ADX thresholds for both entries and exits, or introducing an additional filter based on RSI.

pre_reflection.text:
[Pre-reflection] - The strategy is based on breakout and trend-following principles, but it may struggle to identify trends in ranging markets. This can lead to subpar performance.

- The entry signals are not very clear-cut (close > donchian.upper AND adx.adx > 25), which could result in choppy trading.
[Backup plan] - Try using a more robust trend-following indicator like MACD or RSI instead of ADX. 
- Adjust the breakout parameters to allow for more time in range before reentry.
